steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to describe the format of the poem “15 haiku (for Toni Morrison).” Format means structural composition — how the poem is built — not its theme, publication history, or the author’s reasoning.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The poems in Morning Haiku (2010) are each written as a sequence of haiku.’: Describes the entire book, not the specific poem. Also inaccurate — the notes say only this poem is a sequence of fifteen haiku, not all poems in the collection. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Sanchez chose the form used in the poem because it helps “maintain memory and dignity.”’: Explains Sanchez’s reason for using haiku, not what the format actually is. This is motivation, not description. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The poem “15 haiku (for Toni Morrison)” is written as a sequence of fifteen haiku.’: Directly names the poem and states its structural format — a sequence of fifteen haiku. Matches the task exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘The poem was published in the 2010 book Morning Haiku, which “celebrates the gifts of life and mourns the deaths of revered African American figures.”’: Covers publication and theme, neither of which is format. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
